chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Crl. Misc. Appl. No.527 of 2024 (Syed Anis Haider v. SSP Karachi and others) __________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge __________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on MA No.6577/2024 2. For orders on MA No.6578/2024 3. For hearing of main case 21.05.2024 Applicant in person ========= 1. Urgency granted. 2. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Based on the allegation of injecting some toxic substance to his brother Syed Hassan Haider at the hands of the proposed accused, the applicant by making an application u/s 22-A/B Cr.PC sought direction against SHO PS Sachal to record his FIR; it was dismissed by learned VIth - Additional Sessions Judge/Ex-Officio Justice of Peace Malir Karachi vide order dated 14.05.2024, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court by way of instant Crl. Misc. Application u/s 561-A Cr. PC. It is contended by the applicant that the cognizable offence has taken place, yet his application has been dismissed by Ex-Officio Peace of Justice by way of the impugned order, therefore, the same is to be examined by this Court. Heard arguments and perused the record. There appears to be a dispute between the parties over the payment of utility bills. Syed Hassan Haider the alleged victim has denied the accusation by filing his affidavit before learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace. In these premises, learned Ex-officio Justice of Peace was right to dismiss the application of the applicant by way of the impugned order; it is not found illegal to be interfered with by this Court by way of the instant Crl.

Syed Hassan Haider the alleged victim has denied the accusation by filing his affidavit before learned Ex-Officio Justice of Peace. In these premises, learned Ex-officio Justice of Peace was right to dismiss the application of the applicant by way of the impugned order; it is not found illegal to be interfered with by this Court by way of the instant Crl. Misc. Application u/s 561-A Cr. PC; it is dismissed in limine. J U D G E Nadir*
